justification: All pass criteria met: strong signals explicitly acknowledged ('excellent plumbing', 'observability, circuit breakers, deployment strategy are all strong'); non-idempotent write + retry = guaranteed duplicates identified as the primary blocker with quantified impact (864K duplicates/day); DLQ with no consumer explicitly called 'a data loss path disguised as a resilience feature'; at-least-once + non-idempotent analyzed as 'expected behavior under normal operation'; specific recommendations include idempotency keys and DLQ consumer with alerting; verdict is NOT READY grounded in data correctness; observability strengths explicitly not allowed to compensate ('The team built excellent plumbing... But the service has a data integrity hole at its core').

observations.text: Correctly identified the ACK timeout scenario as the most common duplication path, showing deep understanding of the failure mode
Quantified the duplication impact (0.1% ACK loss = 864K duplicates/day at 10K events/sec) — makes the issue concrete
Framed DLQ as 'a lie' and 'data loss path disguised as a resilience feature' — exactly the right severity
Explicitly acknowledged observability strengths without letting them soften the verdict
Escalations properly routed to Shaq for implementation and MJ for design
Recommendations clearly tiered into blocking must-haves vs should-haves vs nice-to-haves
Identified additional failure modes beyond the rubric (DLQ capacity, backpressure, warehouse write timeout)
